title: Doctor explaining complex surgery options to patient and family
title_zh: 医生向患者及家属解释复杂手术方案

tree.setup: You are a senior neurosurgeon at a top-tier hospital in Beijing. You need to explain a complex brain tumor surgery to a patient and their anxious family. The tumor is in a difficult location near the speech center, and there are multiple treatment options with different risk profiles. The patient's adult daughter is highly educated and asks detailed questions, while the elderly patient's spouse is primarily concerned about quality of life. You must communicate complex medical information clearly while showing empathy.
tree.setup_zh: 你是北京一家三甲医院的资深神经外科医生。你需要向一位患者及其焦虑的家属解释一项复杂的脑肿瘤手术。肿瘤位于语言中枢附近的高风险区域，有多种治疗方案，各自风险不同。患者的成年女儿受过高等教育，会提出详细问题；年迈的配偶则主要关心生活质量。你必须清晰传达复杂的医学信息，同时展现同理心。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 医生，我们一家人都来了。报告出来以后我们都没睡好觉。我父亲脑子里这个东西……到底有多严重？能不能治好？我在网上查了很多资料，但越查越害怕。请您跟我们说实话。
turns.text_pinyin: yīshēng, wǒmen yī jiā rén dōu lái le. bàogào chūlái yǐhòu wǒmen dōu méi shuì hǎo jiào. wǒ fùqīn nǎozi lǐ zhège dōngxī... dàodǐ yǒu duō yánzhòng? néng bù néng zhì hǎo? wǒ zài wǎng shàng chá le hěn duō zīliào, dàn yuè chá yuè hàipà. qǐng nín gēn wǒmen shuō shíhuà.
turns.text_en: Doctor, our whole family is here. None of us have slept well since the report came out. This thing in my father's brain... how serious is it exactly? Can it be cured? I've researched a lot online, but the more I read, the more scared I get. Please be honest with us.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Explain the diagnosis and treatment options clearly and empathetically.

options.text_zh: 这个问题很复杂，我需要更多时间考虑。
options.text_pinyin: Zhège wèntí hěn fùzá, wǒ xūyào gèng duō shíjiān kǎolǜ.
options.text_en: This question is very complex; I need more time to consider it.
options.feedback: Avoids engaging with the specific question at hand.

options.text_zh: 我觉得这不是最重要的问题。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde zhè bú shì zuì zhòngyào de wèntí.
options.text_en: I don't think this is the most important question.
options.feedback: Redirects rather than addressing the topic directly.

options.text_zh: 我理解你们的焦虑，确诊消息对任何家庭来说都是巨大的冲击。请放心，我会把所有信息如实告诉你们，我们一起来面对。先说诊断情况：影像学结果显示，您父亲左侧额颞叶交界处有一个直径约三点五厘米的占位性病变。根据增强核磁的影像特征，我们初步判断为胶质瘤，但具体的病理分级需要术后通过组织活检才能确定。这个位置比较特殊——它靠近布罗卡区，也就是大脑的语言运动中枢。这意味着手术存在一定的语言功能风险，我需要如实告知。但这绝不意味着没有治疗希望。目前有三个方案供你们考虑：第一，在功能磁共振和术中神经电生理监测引导下进行显微手术切除。这是最积极的方案，目标是最大范围安全切除肿瘤。根据我们科室近五年同类手术的统计数据，完全切除率约百分之七十五，术后出现永久性语言障碍的概率约百分之八——而且这百分之八中的大多数是轻度的命名困难，不是完全丧失语言能力。第二，如果家属顾虑手术风险，可以先进行立体定向穿刺活检明确病理分级，然后根据结果决定是放疗、化疗，还是综合治疗。这个方案风险较低，但对肿瘤的控制力度也相对有限。第三，对于一些低级别的胶质瘤，如果肿瘤生长缓慢，也有密切随访观察的选项——但需要每三个月复查核磁。我个人的临床判断是倾向第一方案，因为从影像特征来看，这个肿瘤不像是低级别的。但最终决定权在你们手里，我的职责是确保你们在充分知情的基础上做出选择。
options.text_pinyin: wǒ lǐjiě nǐmen de jiāolǜ, quèzhěn xiāoxi duì rènhé jiātíng lái shuō dōu shì jùdà de chōngjī. qǐng fàngxīn, wǒ huì bǎ suǒyǒu xìnxī rúshí gàosù nǐmen, wǒmen yīqǐ lái miànduì. xiān shuō zhěnduàn qíngkuàng: yǐngxiàng xué jiéguǒ xiǎnshì, nín fùqīn zuǒcè é niè yè jiāojiè chù yǒu yī gè zhíjìng yuē sān diǎn wǔ límǐ de zhànwèi xìng bìngbiàn. gēnjù zēngqiáng hécí de yǐngxiàng tèzhēng, wǒmen chūbù pànduàn wéi jiāozhì liú, dàn jùtǐ de bìnglǐ fēnjí xūyào shùhòu tōngguò zǔzhī huójiǎn cáinéng quèdìng. zhège wèizhì bǐjiào tèshū — tā kàojìn bùluókǎ qū, yě jiùshì dànǎo de yǔyán yùndòng zhōngshū. zhè yìwèi zhe shǒushù cúnzài yīdìng de yǔyán gōngnéng fēngxiǎn, wǒ xūyào rúshí gàozhī. dàn zhè juébù yìwèi zhe méiyǒu zhìliáo xīwàng. mùqián yǒu sān gè fāng'àn gōng nǐmen kǎolǜ: dì yī, zài gōngnéng cígòngzhèn hé shù zhōng shénjīng diàn shēnglǐ jiāncè yǐndǎo xià jìnxíng xiǎnwēi shǒushù qiēchú. zhè shì zuì jījí de fāng'àn, mùbiāo shì zuìdà fànwéi ānquán qiēchú zhǒngliú. gēnjù wǒmen kēshì jìn wǔ nián tóng lèi shǒushù de tǒngjì shùjù, wánquán qiēchú lǜ yuē bǎifēnzhī qīshí wǔ, shùhòu chūxiàn yǒngjiǔ xìng yǔyán zhàng'ài de gàilǜ yuē bǎifēnzhī bā — érqiě zhè bǎifēnzhī bā zhōng de dàduōshù shì qīngdù de mìngmíng kùnnán, bú shì wánquán sàngshī yǔyán nénglì. dì èr, rúguǒ jiāshǔ gùlǜ shǒushù fēngxiǎn, kěyǐ xiān jìnxíng lìtǐ dìngxiàng chuāncì huójiǎn míngquè bìnglǐ fēnjí, ránhòu gēnjù jiéguǒ juédìng shì fàngliáo, huàliáo, háishì zōnghé zhìliáo. zhège fāng'àn fēngxiǎn jiào dī, dàn duì zhǒngliú de kòngzhì lìdù yě xiāngduì yǒuxiàn. dì sān, duìyú yīxiē dī jíbié de jiāozhì liú, rúguǒ zhǒngliú shēngzhǎng huǎnmàn, yě yǒu mìqiè suífǎng guānchá de xuǎnxiàng — dàn xūyào měi sān gè yuè fùchá hécí. wǒ gèrén de línchuáng pànduàn shì qīngxiàng dì yī fāng'àn, yīnwèi cóng yǐngxiàng tèzhēng lái kàn, zhège zhǒngliú bú xiàng shì dī jíbié de. dàn zuìzhōng juédìng quán zài nǐmen shǒu lǐ, wǒ de zhízé shì quèbǎo nǐmen zài chōngfèn zhīqíng de jīchǔ shàng zuòchū xuǎnzé.
options.text_en: I understand your anxiety — a diagnosis like this is an enormous shock for any family. Rest assured, I will share all information honestly, and we'll face this together. Let me start with the diagnosis: imaging results show a space-occupying lesion approximately 3.5 centimeters in diameter at the junction of your father's left frontal and temporal lobes. Based on contrast-enhanced MRI characteristics, our preliminary assessment is glioma, but the specific pathological grade can only be confirmed through post-operative tissue biopsy. This location is quite special — it's near Broca's area, the brain's speech motor center. This means surgery carries a certain risk to language function, which I must honestly disclose. But this absolutely does not mean there's no hope for treatment. Currently there are three options for your consideration: first, microsurgical resection guided by functional MRI and intraoperative neurophysiological monitoring. This is the most aggressive option, aiming for maximum safe resection. Based on our department's statistical data for similar surgeries over the past five years, the complete resection rate is approximately 75%, with a probability of permanent speech impairment of approximately 8% — and most of that 8% involves mild naming difficulties, not complete loss of speech. Second, if the family is concerned about surgical risks, we can first perform a stereotactic needle biopsy to establish the pathological grade, then decide between radiation, chemotherapy, or combined treatment based on results. This option is lower risk but also relatively limited in tumor control. Third, for some low-grade gliomas with slow growth, close follow-up observation is also an option — but requires MRI re-examination every three months. My personal clinical judgment favors the first option, because the imaging characteristics suggest this is unlikely to be low-grade. But the final decision is in your hands — my responsibility is to ensure you make your choice on a fully informed basis.
options.feedback: Medically thorough and emotionally calibrated — uses 占位性病变, 胶质瘤, 布罗卡区, 功能磁共振, 术中神经电生理监测, 立体定向穿刺活检, 病理分级 with specialist precision while maintaining accessible language. The three-option framework respects patient autonomy. Providing department-specific statistics (75% resection rate, 8% speech risk) builds trust through transparency. The empathetic framing (我们一起来面对) and shared decision-making language (最终决定权在你们手里) demonstrate the best of modern medical communication.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: （患者配偶开口）医生，我不懂那些医学术语。我就想知道一件事——如果做了手术，他以后还能正常说话吗？还能认识我们吗？他今年七十三了，我不想让他在最后几年受那么大的罪。如果手术之后他什么都不能做了，那我宁愿让他开开心心地过……
turns.text_pinyin: (huànzhě pèi'ǒu kāikǒu) yīshēng, wǒ bù dǒng nàxiē yīxué shùyǔ. wǒ jiù xiǎng zhīdào yī jiàn shì — rúguǒ zuò le shǒushù, tā yǐhòu hái néng zhèngcháng shuōhuà ma? hái néng rènshi wǒmen ma? tā jīnnián qīshí sān le, wǒ bù xiǎng ràng tā zài zuìhòu jǐ nián shòu nàme dà de zuì. rúguǒ shǒushù zhīhòu tā shénme dōu bù néng zuò le, nà wǒ nìngyuàn ràng tā kāikāixīnxīn de guò...
turns.text_en: (Patient's spouse speaks up) Doctor, I don't understand those medical terms. I just want to know one thing — if he has the surgery, will he still be able to talk normally? Will he still recognize us? He's 73 this year, I don't want him to suffer so much in his last years. If after surgery he can't do anything, then I'd rather let him live happily...

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Address the elderly spouse's quality-of-life concerns with empathy and clarity.

options.text_zh: 阿姨，您说的每一句话我都听到了，而且您考虑的角度非常重要——生活质量有时候比多活几年更重要，这不是一个错误的想法。让我用最简单的话来回答您。关于说话：做完手术后，大约百分之九十二的患者可以正常交流。少数人可能会有一段时间说话不太流利，就像话到嘴边一时想不起来怎么说——但大部分这种情况会在三到六个月内慢慢恢复。关于认人：这个手术不涉及记忆和认知的区域，所以他手术后一定还认识您，认识所有家人。这一点请您放心。但我也想让您考虑另一面——如果不做手术，肿瘤继续生长会逐渐压迫更多的脑组织。到那个时候，失去语言能力和认知功能反而是更大概率的结果。所以手术的目的不仅是延长生命，更是保护他现在拥有的这些能力——包括和您说话、认识家人的能力。当然，最终怎么选，是你们一家人的决定。我们医疗团队会全力支持你们做出的任何选择。如果您需要时间考虑，不用着急——一两周内做出决定都来得及。
options.text_pinyin: āyí, nín shuō de měi yī jù huà wǒ dōu tīngdào le, érqiě nín kǎolǜ de jiǎodù fēicháng zhòngyào — shēnghuó zhìliàng yǒu shíhòu bǐ duō huó jǐ nián gèng zhòngyào, zhè bú shì yī gè cuòwù de xiǎngfǎ. ràng wǒ yòng zuì jiǎndān de huà lái huídá nín. guānyú shuōhuà: zuò wán shǒushù hòu, dàyuē bǎifēnzhī jiǔshí èr de huànzhě kěyǐ zhèngcháng jiāoliú. shǎoshù rén kěnéng huì yǒu yī duàn shíjiān shuōhuà bú tài liúlì, jiù xiàng huà dào zuǐbiān yīshí xiǎng bù qǐlái zěnme shuō — dàn dà bùfèn zhè zhǒng qíngkuàng huì zài sān dào liù gè yuè nèi mànmàn huīfù. guānyú rèn rén: zhège shǒushù bù shèjí jìyì hé rènzhī de qūyù, suǒyǐ tā shǒushù hòu yīdìng hái rènshi nín, rènshi suǒyǒu jiārén. zhè yīdiǎn qǐng nín fàngxīn. dàn wǒ yě xiǎng ràng nín kǎolǜ lìng yī miàn — rúguǒ bú zuò shǒushù, zhǒngliú jìxù shēngzhǎng huì zhújiàn yāpò gèng duō de nǎo zǔzhī. dào nàge shíhòu, shīqù yǔyán nénglì hé rènzhī gōngnéng fǎn'ér shì gèng dà gàilǜ de jiéguǒ. suǒyǐ shǒushù de mùdì bùjǐn shì yáncháng shēngmìng, gèng shì bǎohù tā xiànzài yǒngyǒu de zhèxiē nénglì — bāokuò hé nín shuōhuà, rènshi jiārén de nénglì. dāngrán, zuìzhōng zěnme xuǎn, shì nǐmen yī jiā rén de juédìng. wǒmen yīliáo tuánduì huì quánlì zhīchí nǐmen zuòchū de rènhé xuǎnzé. rúguǒ nín xūyào shíjiān kǎolǜ, búyòng zhāojí — yī liǎng zhōu nèi zuòchū juédìng dōu láidejí.
options.text_en: Auntie, I hear every word you're saying, and the angle you're considering is very important — quality of life is sometimes more important than living a few more years, and that is not a wrong way to think. Let me answer you in the simplest terms. About speaking: after surgery, approximately 92% of patients can communicate normally. A small number may have a period of less fluent speech, like having a word on the tip of your tongue but not quite being able to say it — but most of these cases gradually recover within three to six months. About recognition: this surgery doesn't involve the areas for memory and cognition, so he will definitely still recognize you after surgery, recognize all the family members. Please rest assured on this point. But I also want you to consider the other side — if surgery isn't performed, the tumor's continued growth will gradually compress more brain tissue. At that point, losing speech and cognitive function becomes a higher probability outcome. So the purpose of surgery isn't just to extend life, but to protect the abilities he has now — including the ability to talk to you and recognize family. Of course, the final choice is your family's decision. Our medical team will fully support whatever choice you make. If you need time to think, there's no rush — making a decision within one to two weeks is still in time.
options.feedback: Emotionally masterful — validates the spouse's quality-of-life perspective before reframing surgery as protective rather than aggressive. Using 话到嘴边想不起来 to explain aphasia makes medical concepts accessible to a non-specialist. The key insight — that without surgery, the very outcomes she fears (loss of speech, recognition) become more likely — is gently but clearly conveyed. Calling her 阿姨 and affirming 生活质量有时候比多活几年更重要 demonstrates genuine empathy. Offering unhurried decision time reduces pressure and builds trust.

options.text_zh: 这个问题很复杂，我需要更多时间考虑。
options.text_pinyin: Zhège wèntí hěn fùzá, wǒ xūyào gèng duō shíjiān kǎolǜ.
options.text_en: This question is very complex; I need more time to consider it.
options.feedback: Avoids engaging with the specific question at hand.

options.text_zh: 我觉得这不是最重要的问题。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde zhè bú shì zuì zhòngyào de wèntí.
options.text_en: I don't think this is the most important question.
options.feedback: Redirects rather than addressing the topic directly.

tree.cultural_note: Medical communication in Chinese hospitals involves navigating family dynamics where decisions are often collective. The patient's spouse (老伴) and adult children frequently participate in treatment decisions together. Addressing elderly family members as 阿姨/叔叔 conveys warmth and respect. Chinese medical ethics has evolved toward 知情同意 (informed consent) and shared decision-making, though the traditional pattern of 家长式医疗 (paternalistic medicine) — where doctors simply tell families what to do — is still common. The most skilled physicians balance technical precision (using terms like 布罗卡区, 胶质瘤, 功能磁共振) with emotional accessibility, especially when families include both medically literate and non-specialist members.
